metadata.title: How do small councils compare to the state?
metadata.subtitle: Small councils are often assumed to sit behind the broader sector. The real picture is more uneven - and more useful.
metadata.caveat: All values in this standalone prototype are mock, illustrative placeholder values created for product demonstration only. They are not governed outputs and must not be used for decisions.
metadata.futureSourcePath: Replace this file with report-ready JSON produced from governed datamarts and a report manifest.

reportManifest.title: Small Council vs State Scrollytelling Prototype
reportManifest.report_title_candidate: How do small councils compare to the state?
reportManifest.metric_definition: Weekly range midpoint rates by standard band, comparing an illustrative small-council cohort median with an illustrative statewide median.

payPointGalaxy.question: What does the whole pay field look like over time?
payPointGalaxy.title: Pay point galaxy
payPointGalaxy.summary: Illustrative placeholder points showing how statewide and small-council average weekly values can be plotted across effective dates, bands, and level labels.

summaryMetrics.label: Galaxy points
summaryMetrics.detail: cohort x date x band averages

payPointGalaxy.caveat: Illustrative placeholder data only. Not for decision-making.

cohort.title: Small councils as an analytical choice
cohort.definition: For this prototype, small councils are represented by a rural/regional illustrative cohort used to test report structure and visual logic.
cohort.smallCouncilDefinition: Councils with lower population scale and rural/regional service context, expressed here as a placeholder cohort.
cohort.statewideDefinition: All Victorian councils in the report universe.
cohort.productionCaveat: Production reporting should bind this section to governed council classification and cohort metadata, not handwritten membership.

evidenceChain.description: Enterprise agreement and source document records are registered with canonical council identity.
Pay, uplift, classification, and entitlement candidates are extracted with source references.
Human QA and governance status separate accepted facts from staged or blocked records.
Metric-aware marts reshape governed records into comparable analytical grains.
A manifest-backed report view carries caveats, sources, cohorts, metrics, and intended use.

payByBand.question: Do small councils pay less?

bands.label: Band 1
bands.note: Entry bands show a narrow indicative difference.

bands.label: Band 2
bands.note: Lower bands sit close enough that local classification mix could change the reading.

bands.label: Band 3
bands.note: A visible difference appears in the middle of the structure.

bands.label: Band 4
bands.note: Band 4 begins to show meaningful exposure in this mock set.

bands.label: Band 5
bands.note: The largest current-period gap appears at Band 5, but scheduled uplifts affect the horizon.

bands.label: Band 6
bands.note: Band 6 looks exposed today and is sensitive to agreement cycle timing.

bands.label: Band 7
bands.note: Senior bands are not uniformly lower in the placeholder data.

bands.label: Band 8
bands.note: The upper band gap is narrow enough that agreement age and role mix would matter.

state_only.label: Statewide median
state_only.summary: Start with the statewide median as the reference line.

small_vs_state.label: Small vs state
small_vs_state.summary: Layer the small-council cohort median over the statewide pattern.

gap_highlight.label: Largest gaps
gap_highlight.summary: Highlight the bands where the placeholder cohort trails by the largest weekly amount.

band_focus.label: Narrow gaps
band_focus.summary: Some bands sit close to the statewide median, changing the executive story.

timing_sensitive.label: Timing sensitive
timing_sensitive.summary: Bands exposed today may move after scheduled uplifts.

takeaway.label: Takeaway
takeaway.summary: The useful question is where exposure exists, not whether every band is lower.

payByBand.takeaway: In the placeholder data, small councils trail most clearly in Bands 5 and 6. Lower and upper bands are closer, which makes workforce shape and timing essential context.

distribution.question: Is the gap uniform, or does it depend where you look?
distribution.summary: The statewide spread is wide enough that the small-council median can sit below the state median while still overlapping much of the sector range.
distribution.caveat: Illustrative percentile ribbons only. Production views should use pay_distribution_point_mart and declare the comparison metric.

classificationContext.question: Are we comparing the same workforce shape?
classificationContext.summary: Band-level comparison controls one source of noise, but workforce mix still matters. A council concentrated in Bands 3-5 can experience a different labour-market story from a council concentrated in Bands 6-8.
classificationContext.productionNote: Production reporting should distinguish band-level pay comparison from workforce composition and classification mapping quality.

upliftTimeline.question: Is this a structural pay gap, or a timing artefact?
upliftTimeline.summary: Snapshot comparisons can mislead when councils sit at different points in their agreement cycle.

phases.label: Current snapshot
phases.description: Compare current weekly rates before scheduled increases are applied.

phases.label: Scheduled uplifts
phases.description: Reveal upcoming percentage increases and the agreement-cycle position.

phases.label: Horizon view
phases.description: Recalculate the comparison after scheduled uplifts land.

upliftTimeline.caveat: Mock uplift rules only. Production reporting should connect to uplift_timing_mart and temporal_pay_movement_mart.

entitlements.question: Do small councils compete differently?
entitlements.summary: Pay position is only one part of employment value. Small councils may compete through leave, flexibility, progression, or allowances where direct pay position is harder to move.

Pay.label: Below median
Pay.note: Band 5-6 exposure in placeholder pay data.

Leave.label: Near state
Leave.note: No governed leave conclusion in prototype.

entitlements.caveat: Indicative prototype view. Placeholder entitlement scoring is designed to connect to governed entitlement summaries later.

executiveTakeaways.title: Exposure
executiveTakeaways.body: The placeholder cohort is most exposed in mid-band professional and supervisory roles, not uniformly across the structure.

executiveTakeaways.title: Resilience
executiveTakeaways.body: Narrow gaps at the lower and upper ends mean the narrative should avoid a blanket 'small councils pay less' conclusion.

executiveTakeaways.title: Timing risk
executiveTakeaways.body: Agreement-cycle timing can change the apparent gap, so current snapshots need a horizon view before executive interpretation.

narrativeSteps.title: Start with the state
narrativeSteps.body: The statewide median gives the executive reader a reference line. It is the start of the argument, not the answer.

narrativeSteps.title: Layer the small-council cohort
narrativeSteps.body: The placeholder small-council median sits below the state in most bands, but the distance changes materially across the structure.

narrativeSteps.title: The largest gaps are concentrated
narrativeSteps.body: Bands 5 and 6 show the clearest indicative exposure. This is where workforce pressure, recruitment difficulty, and classification mapping deserve attention.

narrativeSteps.title: Some bands are much closer
narrativeSteps.body: Lower and upper bands are closer to the statewide benchmark. A useful report separates broad positioning from the precise bands that matter.

narrativeSteps.title: The reading is sensitive to agreement age
narrativeSteps.body: Where scheduled uplifts are near, today's gap may partly reflect timing rather than underlying pay strategy.

narrativeSteps.title: The useful answer is conditional
narrativeSteps.body: Small councils do not simply sit below the state. Their position depends on band, spread, workforce shape, and the point in the agreement cycle.
